# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# CloudFormation template creates a role with an IAM Policy that allows cross-account access 
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09" # required version of yaml syntax, it's not recommended to use this later. This is for AWS documentation purpose only and can be removed or modified as per your needs but should remain consistent with the template below versions upto 
Parameters:                                  # Start parameters section (requires at least one parameter)  
    AccountIds:                             # Input Parameter Section, each value must match a string. Multiple values are allowed here if you prefer. Also note that multiple account Ids can be passed in this manner or via different input files/parameters when the template is executed – it's just required to use only one for simplicity
        Type: String                            # Required Parameter, also requires a string type value because AWS SSM parameter stores require strings as values. All of your account id should be passed in this way while calling aws-cli or any other CLI tool that you are using with these accounts which can use it to pass multiple comma separated list(by -a and --account)
        Default: "acct1, acct2"             # default value for the parameter. Multiple account id's should be passed as a single string (comma seperated).  If not provided in template or when calling aws CLI tool it will use these defaults to create stack . e-g.: --account accnt1,accnt2
        Description: "List of Account Ids"     # optional description. This can be useful for understanding what the parameter represents and its purpose – not required but very recommended   - AUTOMATED by CloudFormation AWS provider service or when you describe a resource using 'aws cloudformation describe-resources --query Resources[*].PhysicalResourceId'
Resources:                                 # Start resources section (provides at least one statement)   
    IAMRoleForCrossAccountAccessWithTrustPolicyAllowingAssumptionsFromMultipleAWSOrganizationsAcccountsInOrg: 
        Type: "AWS::IAM::Role"     # Required Resource type, for AWS service to be created.   It'll create the resource (an Iam Role) with your defined attributes like name and parameters in this case - it should match exactly what you have specified above at top of yaml file
        Properties:               # Define all properties that are used by AWSTemplateFormatVersion 2 template version. For example, if there is any security group then we define a resource to reference the same or similar defined component in Security Group below and many more   -AUTOMATED BY CLOUDFORMATION AWS PROVIDER
            AssumeCrossAccountAccess: "true"                # This property needs boolean value – it's for allowing access from multiple accounts. The name of this attribute should match exactly with the one specified above at top section like 'AssumeCrossAccountAssion', ie., a comma-separated list (can be passed in --account and -a command line option)
            Policies:                  # Required if we want to define any IAM policies. Here, for cross account access grant the trust policy is defined which allows all AWS accounts or other specified ACCOUNT IDs who can assume this role via AssumeRolePolicyStatement under Permissions section of iam resource type     - AUTOMATED BY CLOUDFORMATION SERVICE
                Type: "AWS::IAM::ManagedPolicy"              # Required Resource types – Manifest and IAM policies needs to be defined here. AWS automatically provides managed policy if you create a role in your cloud environment, so we use the type of this resource which is - AWSTemplateFormatVersion2 template version requires one Type attribute for every supported provider (EC2/Lambda etc), with its values being string types or references specified above by Parameters
                Properties:                             # Define all properties that are used in AWS IAM policies section.  In case of Trust policy iam role allow assumptions from multiple accounts can be defined here - AUTOMATED BY CLOUDFORMATION SERVICE        
                    Policies:                              # Required if we want to define any permissions on the trustee's account id in this IAM policies section. Here, for allowing access by one of more ACCOUNT IDS then a Allow statement will be defined here with that specific AccountId and ARN as Principal    - AUTOMATED BY CLOUDFORMATION SERVICE
                        - 
                            Action: "sts:AssumeRole"                 # Define the actions allowed in this policy. Here, we are allowing Assume role action so sts::AssumeROLE is defined under permittedactions here and a Principal with arn of specific account id will be provided as Permissions    - AUTOMATED BY CLOUDFORMATION SERVICE
                            Effect: "Allow"                          # This property defines the effect for this statement – it'll allow or deny in AWS managed template version. Here, Allow is used because we are allowing Access and Define policies to others roles   -- (can be Deny also). If you define anything else then only that action will allowed otherwise not
                            Principal:                             # Required if the IAM policy defines permissions on a principal or group    - AUTOMATED BY CLOUDFORMATION SERVICE – this should match with what is set up in iam role which we are creating. All ACCOUNT IDs can be passed using Parameters
                                Type: String                          # Required Principal attribute, it'll accept string type values like account id or arn to the specific principal     - AUTOMATED BY CLOUDFORMATION SERVICE – this should match with what is set up in iam role which we are creating. All ACCOUNT IDs can be passed using Parameters
                                Default: "acct1, acct2"                # This account id(default value) for each principal if not provided while calling aws cli tool it uses these default values to create stack – e-g.: --account accnt1.aws etc., All ACCOUNT IDS can be passed using Parameters
